pullRequest.title: Fix ZeroDivisionError for np.mean([])
pullRequest.body:
closes https://github.com/numba/numba/issues/5502

re-opened for CI to run (original PR: #10460)

pullRequest.title: fix `RecursionError` on large, generated CFGs
pullRequest.body:
Fixes #5611

We convert the recursive implementation of the `_find_topo_order` function to a stack based one. This fixes previously encountered issues when attempting to compile large synthetically generated programs. The Control Flow Graph of these programs would become so large that this implementation would hit the Python interpreter recursion limit.

pullRequest.title: Support for np.moveaxis
pullRequest.body:
Closes #7369 

The helper `normalize_axis_tuple` could be an overload for `numpy.lib.array_utils.normalize_axis_tuple`, do you want it to be? I think it only has that location/ public API since numpy 2.x, not sure if numba minds that.

pullRequest.title: Added traceback printing capability while NRT debugging
pullRequest.body:
Resolves #8559

As titled, 
This PR adds traceback printing capability while NRT debugging is enabled. 

Also adds a flag that helps user configure the stack limit for traceback. This functionality can be disabled by setting the same stack limit to zero.

An example NRT Debug with stack limit as 3:

```python
import numpy as np
import numba

@numba.njit
def foo(x):
    return x + 5

x = np.array([0,1,0])
foo(x)
```

Outputs the follows:
```
*** NRT_Decref 1 [0x3245f80]
Traceback:  File "/home/kaustubh/Desktop/WorkSpaces/Numba Workspace/nrt_debug/numba/numba/core/callwrapper.py", line 180, in build_wrapper
    cleanup_manager.emit_cleanup()
  File "/home/kaustubh/Desktop/WorkSpaces/Numba Workspace/nrt_debug/numba/numba/core/callwrapper.py", line 71, in emit_cleanup
    dtor()
  File "/home/kaustubh/Desktop/WorkSpaces/Numba Workspace/nrt_debug/numba/numba/core/callwrapper.py", line 50, in cleanup_arg
    self.context.nrt.decref(self.builder, ty, native.value)

*** NRT_Decref 2 [0x326b5f0]
Traceback:  File "/home/kaustubh/Desktop/WorkSpaces/Numba Workspace/nrt_debug/numba/numba/core/pythonapi.py", line 1405, in from_native_return
    out = self.from_native_value(typ, val, env_manager)
  File "/home/kaustubh/Desktop/WorkSpaces/Numba Workspace/nrt_debug/numba/numba/core/pythonapi.py", line 1419, in from_native_value
    return impl(typ, val, c)
  File "/home/kaustubh/Desktop/WorkSpaces/Numba Workspace/nrt_debug/numba/numba/core/boxing.py", line 432, in box_array
    c.context.nrt.decref(c.builder, typ, val)
```

pullRequest.body:
<!--

Thanks for wanting to contribute to Numba :)

First, if you need some help or want to chat to the core developers, please
visit https://gitter.im/numba/numba for real time chat or post to the Numba
forum https://numba.discourse.group/.

Here's some guidelines to help the review process go smoothly.

0. Please write a description in this text box of the changes that are being

1. Please ensure that you have written units tests for the changes made/features

2. If you are closing an issue please use one of the automatic closing words as
   noted here: https://help.github.com/articles/closing-issues-using-keywords/

3. If your pull request is not ready for review but you want to make use of the
   continuous integration testing facilities here, please click the arrow besides
   "Create Pull Request" and choose "Create Draft Pull Request".
   When it's ready for review, you can click the button "ready to review" near
   the end of the pull request
   (besides "This pull request is still a work in progress".)
   The maintainers will then be automatically notified to review it.

4. Once review has taken place please do not add features or make changes out of
   the scope of those requested by the reviewer (doing this just add delays as
   already reviewed code ends up having to be re-reviewed/it is hard to tell
   what is new etc!). Further, please do not rebase your branch on main/force
   push/rewrite history, doing any of these causes the context of any comments
   made by reviewers to be lost. If conflicts occur against main they should
   be resolved by merging main into the branch used for making the pull
   request.

Many thanks in advance for your cooperation!

-->

Closes https://github.com/numba/numba/issues/8945

Here's a simple test

```python
import numpy as np
import numba


@numba.jit(cache=False)
def hello():
    buffer = np.arange(1024 * 1024, 1024 * 1024 + 1024, dtype=np.int64)
    buffer2 = np.frombuffer(buffer, dtype=np.int64)
    buffer3 = np.frombuffer(buffer, dtype=np.int64, count=5, offset=1)
    print(buffer)
    print(buffer2)
    print(buffer3)

    assert len(buffer3) == 5
    assert np.all(buffer[1:6] == buffer3)


hello()
```


Interestingly...this fails?

```python
import numpy as np
import numba


@numba.jit(cache=False)
def hello():
    buffer = np.empty(1024, dtype=np.float64)
    buffer2 = np.frombuffer(buffer, dtype=np.float64)
    buffer3 = np.frombuffer(buffer, dtype=np.float64, count=5, offset=1)
    print(buffer)
    print(buffer2)
    print(buffer3)

    assert len(buffer3) == 5
    assert np.all(buffer[1:6] == buffer3)


hello()

```

pullRequest.title: Handle empty arrays in np.median implementation and add datetime check
pullRequest.body:
Fixes #9433 

Added a check to handle empty arrays by returning np.nan for non-datetime arrays.

pullRequest.title: Fix #9585: Correct exceptions raised by NumPy dtype typing and catch them
pullRequest.body:
This should address inconsistencies in the exceptions raised by `numpy_support.from_dtype()` and ensure that we catch its exceptions.

I did consider whether we ever need to catch a `ValueError` - perhaps we should only be catching `NumbaValueError` instances. However, some experiments with only catching `NumbaValueError` and not just `ValueError` resulted in enough failures in the test suite (unpleasant looking tracebacks, segfaults in parfors tests) even after making a bunch of fixups that I think trying to exclusively rely on `NumbaValueError` is a larger, separate, nice-to-have task rather than something that needs resolving as part of this particular issue.

Fixes #9585.

pullRequest.title: Fix #9931 integer power precision issue for python3.11
pullRequest.body:
closes #9931 

The root cause and reason for fix is as described on the issue comment- https://github.com/numba/numba/issues/9931#issuecomment-3747593783

pullRequest.title: Ensure _launch_threads is called if cached function calls a parallel=True function.
pullRequest.body:
Resolves #9938 

Put reload_init from compilation state into code library and then merge called function's reload_init into the parent function.  This process is naturally recursive as the compilation process is naturally recursive so reload_init's from called functions now percolate up to their callee's.

pullRequest.title: Fixed invert in overload and added testcase.
pullRequest.body:
Fixed #10039

Added __invert__ method in jitclass and written testcase.

pullRequest.title: Docs: Fix jit decorator parameter name from 'signature' to 'signature_or_function'
pullRequest.body:
This PR fixes issue #10049 where users attempting to use `@numba.jit(signature='f4(f4)')` 
receive a KeyError about 'signature' being an unrecognized option.

The parameter is actually named 'signature_or_function' in the implementation, but was 
incorrectly documented as 'signature' in the docs. This PR updates all references in 
the documentation to use the correct parameter name.

Fixes #10049

pullRequest.title: Fix C++ linkage for Python builds with Valgrind
pullRequest.body:
Fedora-based distributions including RHEL and CentOS are building Python with `--with-valgrind` support. The option enables dynamic annotation support. Numba fails to build from soure on systems with Valgrind support. The problem is caused by use of internal CPython core APIs that are untested under C++.

```
g++ -fno-strict-overflow -Wsign-compare -DDYNAMIC_ANNOTATIONS_ENABLED=1 -DNDEBUG -fcf-protection -fexceptions -fcf-protection -fexceptions -fcf-protection -fexceptions -O3 -fPIC -I/tmp/pip-build-env-bi8g7ilp/normal/lib64/python3.13/site-packages/numpy/_core/include -I/opt/app-root/include -I/usr/include/python3.13 -c numba/_dispatcher.cpp -o build/temp.linux-x86_64-cpython-313/numba/_dispatcher.o -std=c++11
  In file included from /usr/include/python3.13/internal/pycore_pythread.h:11,
                   from /usr/include/python3.13/internal/pycore_condvar.h:8,
                   from /usr/include/python3.13/internal/pycore_gil.h:11,
                   from /usr/include/python3.13/internal/pycore_ceval_state.h:12,
                   from /usr/include/python3.13/internal/pycore_interp.h:15,
                   from numba/_dispatcher.cpp:46:
  /usr/include/python3.13/dynamic_annotations.h:472:3: error: template with C linkage
    472 |   template <class T>
        |   ^~~~~~~~
  /usr/include/python3.13/internal/pycore_pythread.h:4:1: note: ‘extern "C"’ linkage started here
      4 | extern "C" {
        | ^~~~~~~~~~
  error: command '/usr/bin/g++' failed with exit code 1
```

- `numba/_dispatcher.cpp` is compiled as C++ program
- the C++ file includes `pycore_pythread.h`
- `pycore_pythread.h` includes `dynamic_annotations.h` in an `extern "C" {}` block.
- `dynamic_annotations.h` gets confused because it is treated as external C header but has `__cplusplus` defined as the same time.
- since `DYNAMIC_ANNOTATIONS_ENABLED != 0 && defined(__cplusplus)` is true, `dynamic_annotations.h` uses C++ features.

This workaround includes `dynamic_annotations.h` earlier and under C++ linkage.

Fixes: #10067

pullRequest.title: fix: handle nan correctly in np.unique
pullRequest.body:
Fixes #10069

This PR updates the `np.unique` overload to handle `np.nan` values, as simply equating `np.nan` does not work -
```py
>>> np.nan == np.nan
False
```

I am not sure if the solution I wrote is the most appropriate, but happy to improve it through reviews.

pullRequest.title: fix/handle curly braces paths
pullRequest.body:
This PR fixes a KeyError that occurs during error message formatting when Numba is installed in Windows paths containing curly braces, such as virtualized environments with UUIDs like C:\Users\{uuid}.
resolves: https://github.com/numba/numba/issues/10094

Changes-
- replace preformatted/f-strings with placeholder-based calls where appropriate
- condition on `_format_msg` used by errors context to return fmt unchanged when no args provided

Added tests to `numba/tests/test_errorhandling.py` to -
- test `_format_msg` when called with placeholder with brace-containing value
- test `_format_msg` when called with preformatted message with braces and no args

pullRequest.title: fix: zero dim index error in ndenumerate
pullRequest.body: Resolves #10127. Intentionally added fix only for contiguous arrays as 0d arrays are always contiguous.

pullRequest.title: Fix Azure failure by reducing memory use.
pullRequest.body:
Fixes: https://github.com/numba/numba/issues/10161

Particularly on Windows and znver3 CPU

Changes:

- Add `_NUMBA_REDUCED_TESTING` env-var in tests to limit test cases, mainly to reduce memory used by the compiler.
- `test_np_functions.py` uses the above env-var to limit tests that has a high memory use. 
- `_NUMBA_REDUCED_TESTING=1` is applied to Windows Azure CI jobs only because the OS is unstable at high memory pressure. On GHA, the Windows workers are getting 2x the RAM and has not experienced any problem.
- `NUMBA_CPU_NAME=generic` is applied to Windows Azure CI jobs only because of high memory pressure issues and that LLVM is creating [massive code bloat on `znver3` CPUs](https://github.com/numba/llvmlite/issues/1247). This issue should be fixed by LLVM >=19.1.
- Linux and MacOS are unchanged.

pullRequest.title: Fix GHA windows workers memory pressure issue
pullRequest.body:
Fixes: https://github.com/numba/numba/pull/10177

Also:
- adds `numba.misc.memoryutils` for tracking future debugging. The memory use is printed at the end of the testsuite.
- https://github.com/numba/numba/pull/10176/commits/22d1da125b9df466898f4761a696f8cf8e3c349c fixes the WinError 10106

pullRequest.title: Fix: add scalar handling for `np.all` overload
pullRequest.body:
resolves https://github.com/numba/numba/issues/10221

`np.all` overload is missing scalar handling and also no `TypingError` is raised. The current implementation of overload does not expect scalar values and errors at lowering -
```
TypeError: Invalid store of i1* to i8* in <numba.core.datamodel.old_models.NdIter object at 0x11c1ae350> (trying to write member #4)
```
This PR adds handling for scalar values of type `types.Number` and `types.Boolean`.

The patch adds support for inputs typed as `types.Number` and `types.Boolean` to `np.all` in addition to `types.Array`. 
That means inputs like -
`0.2` or `np.float64(0.2)`,
`'someString' == 0` (`types.Boolean`)

For all else, `np_all` would throw appropriate `TypingError`.

pullRequest.title: Fix `np.unique` not supporting non-numeric types
pullRequest.body:
Fix #10250.

Use `not (x == x)` instead of `np.isnan` so it works for non-numeric type.

pullRequest.title: GHA/ fix numba `osx-arm64` wheel tag regression
pullRequest.body:
resolves: https://github.com/numba/numba/issues/10264

previous PR merge - https://github.com/numba/numba/pull/10230 changed `osx-arm64` build job to use `actions/setup-python` action to use system python. This change lead to `osx-arm64` wheel builder producing `macosx_10_9_universal2` tagged wheels, which meant they were built with 2 slices (to work on both `x86_64` and `arm64`) which was unintentional.

The root cause of this was found to be GHA systems' interpreter, which was built using deployment target 10.9 and universal2 tagged. This in turn affects wheel built using the interpreter. It also does not obey `MACOSX_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET=11.0` specification from environment.

To correct this, this PR reverts to using conda based python to build `osx-arm64` wheels. It also adds explicit `MACOSX_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET=11.0` specification to build env.

pullRequest.title: gha/ fix `osx-arm64` wheel build for openmp linkage
pullRequest.body:
Fixes: https://github.com/numba/numba/issues/10265

commit - https://github.com/numba/numba/commit/61247ba4606a74da3cd218b7ecf06e2ec17dac2b changed the build process to use system compilers instead of conda compilers. That caused build to not detect openmp and not build `omppool` extension. 
Apple explicitly disabled OpenMP support in compilers that they ship with Xcode.
https://mac.r-project.org/openmp/

To resolve, this PR -
- added `-Xclang -fopenmp` to properly enable OpenMP with Apple's clang
- replace conda install with direct tarball download and extraction of `llvm-openmp` package. This approach provides build-time OpenMP headers and libraries without requiring conda.

pullRequest.title: add support for `math.exp2`
pullRequest.body:
First contribution to numba, would appreciate all feedback.

Resolves #10270.

pullRequest.title: Fix Gufunc leak when called from jit function
pullRequest.body:
Fix #10287 


The bug: 

Gufunc loop nest increment refcount on operand array slices. But the loopnest never releases the refcounts. In fact, the loop-nest does not handle references and it does not have to since gufunc cannot capture references. This patch simply remove the `impl_ret_borrowed` call since it will do incref.

pullRequest.body: resolves: #10293

pullRequest.body:
Maybe fixes: https://github.com/numba/numba/issues/10319

Thanks for wanting to contribute to Numba :)

First, if you need some help or want to chat to the core developers, please
visit https://gitter.im/numba/numba for real time chat or post to the Numba
forum https://numba.discourse.group/.

Here's some guidelines to help the review process go smoothly.

0. Please write a description in this text box of the changes that are being
   made.

1. Please ensure that you have written units tests for the changes made/features
   added.

2. If you are closing an issue please use one of the automatic closing words as
   noted here: https://help.github.com/articles/closing-issues-using-keywords/

3. If your pull request is not ready for review but you want to make use of the
   continuous integration testing facilities here, please click the arrow besides
   "Create Pull Request" and choose "Create Draft Pull Request".
   When it's ready for review, you can click the button "ready to review" near
   the end of the pull request
   (besides "This pull request is still a work in progress".)
   The maintainers will then be automatically notified to review it.

4. Once review has taken place please do not add features or make changes out of
   the scope of those requested by the reviewer (doing this just add delays as
   already reviewed code ends up having to be re-reviewed/it is hard to tell
   what is new etc!). Further, please do not rebase your branch on main/force
   push/rewrite history, doing any of these causes the context of any comments
   made by reviewers to be lost. If conflicts occur against main they should
   be resolved by merging main into the branch used for making the pull
   request.

Many thanks in advance for your cooperation!

-->

pullRequest.body:

resolves #10370

pullRequest.title: Fix swapped shapes in slice assignment error message - Fixes #10402
pullRequest.body:
Fixes #10402

## Problem
The error message had the shapes swapped.
When assigning a (47,) array into a (48,) slice, it incorrectly said:
  "cannot assign slice of shape (47,) from input of shape (48,)"

## Fix
Swapped shape_str and index_str in arrayobj.py.
Now correctly says:
  "cannot assign slice of shape (48,) from input of shape (47,)"

This matches NumPy semantics: "slice" = destination, "input" = source.

pullRequest.title: Updated int_ to map to int64 instead of `ctypes.c_long`
pullRequest.body:
Resolves https://github.com/numba/numba/issues/10524

As titled, 

This PR maps the `int_` type to `ctypes.c_int64` instead of `ctypes.c_long` since the NumPy `int_` datatype actually alaises NumPy `int64` datatype
